title: N2 Mines Bombs Missiles Depth Charges
display_title: N2 Mines Bombs Missiles Depth Charges
wikitext:
[]: N2 Mines/Bombs/Missiles/Depth Charges             Quote Post by Kyoko Sano » Wed Feb 20, 2008 10:23 am N2 Weaponry, is it comparable in descructivness to a nuke?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Wed Feb 20, 2008 10:34 am http://evageeks.org/FGP/N2_Mine They are "the strongest conventional weapon ever made" ; they are a "clean bomb" in the sense that the physical reaction they use leaves no fallout. I interpreted this to mean that they ARE more powerful than ICBMs and such, but they're more "focused"; most of a nuke's energy is lost and directed outwards. That's why its more effecient to launch many moderately sized warheads and not one huge one. My view is that its a REALLY focused blast, but YES it will wipe out a mountain if you set it off. (When Rei uses an N2 Bomb in Episode 19 inside the GeoFront, she says she's using her AT Field to muffle the blast so it doesn't kill everyone in Nerv HQ)

[]:             Quote Post by Kyoko Sano » Wed Feb 20, 2008 10:36 am Ok good, can you lead me to a supplier? My backyard needs remodeling.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Wed Feb 20, 2008 10:44 am I'm sure i remember a thread on this...can't find it though. They, seem to be identical without producing fallout in theory, but the damage is as dynamic as the eva's size. Your sig makes an interesting pun ITT.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Feb 20, 2008 10:55 am ZapalacX wrote: the damage is as dynamic as the eva's size.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Wed Feb 20, 2008 11:59 am good eyes zugz, as always.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Feb 20, 2008 12:50 pm V wrote: When Rei uses an N2 Bomb in Episode 19 inside the GeoFront, she says she's using her AT Field to muffle the blast so it doesn't kill everyone in Nerv HQ

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Wed Feb 20, 2008 3:17 pm For all intents and purposes, the N^2 bomb is a conventional hydrogen fusion munition sans the uranium jacket typically fitted in order to replicate the toxic fallout effect of the original fission nukes and dressed up with meaningless technobabble in order to assuage the Japanese phobia of everything militarily nuclear.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Feb 20, 2008 3:48 pm V wrote: My view is that its a REALLY focused blast, but YES it will wipe out a mountain if you set it off. (When Rei uses an N2 Bomb in Episode 19 inside the GeoFront, she says she's using her AT Field to muffle the blast so it doesn't kill everyone in Nerv HQ)

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Feb 20, 2008 6:57 pm IIRC, thermonuclear bombs actually produce less EMP than ordinary fission weapons. That dubious point aside, a fusion weapon would produce a ton of gamma radiation that'd perforate everything nearby. Most gamma rays are caught inside the extremely dense interior of the sun and convert to lower-energy electromagnetic radiation, which is why we aren't constantly irradiated.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Wed Feb 20, 2008 7:27 pm Out of curiosity, when was the last time you checked the scene (or even a transcript of the scene) in question? While Rei is attempting to penetrate Zeruel's ATF (with bomb in Zero's hand), she says, "ATF full power". (Or something very similar.) Logical conclusion: She's trying to "pump up" the power of Eva-00's ATF so she can neutralize Zeruel's ATF and thereupon attack the core with the N2 bomb.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Wed Feb 20, 2008 11:42 pm oh...i thought she was using the field to stop Zeruel from trying to attack her or something...guess I'm waaay off.

[]:             Quote Post by majlund » Thu Feb 21, 2008 4:30 am V wrote: I interpreted this to mean that they ARE more powerful than ICBMs and such, but they're more "focused"; most of a nuke's energy is lost and directed outwards.

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Thu Feb 21, 2008 5:41 am majlund wrote: V wrote: I interpreted this to mean that they ARE more powerful than ICBMs and such, but they're more "focused"; most of a nuke's energy is lost and directed outwards.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Feb 21, 2008 6:18 am V wrote: I took Rei's "AT Field to full power" line to mean that she's muffling that blast as not to kill everyone.

[]:             Quote Post by Soljer13 » Thu Feb 21, 2008 11:09 am I'd Like to see Sachiel live through getting hit by the Original Tzar Bomba(Emperor Bomb). A RDS-220 Hydrogen Bomb with a yeild of 100 megatons (the one used in real life was only half), This thing Remodeled the peninsula it was tested on, and the shockwaves went around the world 3 times and mesured 5.56 on the rictcher scale.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Thu Feb 21, 2008 4:13 pm Reichu wrote: V wrote: I took Rei's "AT Field to full power" line to mean that she's muffling that blast as not to kill everyone.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Feb 21, 2008 4:29 pm V wrote: Reichu wrote: V wrote: I took Rei's "AT Field to full power" line to mean that she's muffling that blast as not to kill everyone.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Thu Feb 21, 2008 5:33 pm what's the current theory then on how the N2 blast inside the GeoFront didn't fry Shinji and Kaji?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Feb 21, 2008 6:00 pm For the same reason that main characters are never damaged by N2 weapon detonations. (See e.g. episode 01.) Interpreting Rei's line is extremely straightforward , V.* The rest you roll with. Kind of like Misato's car. * Xard's right that your interpretation makes no sense anyway. What's the point of trying to muffle the blast you're hoping will destroy somebody who plans on wiping out your entire species? I mean, come on .
